research.product: Okta Workforce Identity Cloud — System Log API
research.interface: GET /api/v1/logs, Okta Core management REST API (official OpenAPI spec 2026.07.1)

official_sources.title: System Log query | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: polling vs bounded request semantics (polling = ASCENDING with empty/absent until, always returns a next link, ordered by persistence time); since/after mutually exclusive; after is system-generated for next links and must not be crafted; guidance to always follow next links instead of manually paginating with since/until; Link rel=self/rel=next header meaning; filter expression syntax (eventType eq "user.session.start")

official_sources.title: okta/okta-management-openapi-spec — Okta Admin Management API (version 2026.07.1)
official_sources.used_for: listLogEvents operation: query params since/until/after/filter/q/limit (default 100, 0..1000)/sortOrder (ASCENDING default); security SSWS apiToken or oauth2 okta.logs.read; LogEvent schema fields uuid/published/eventType/severity/displayMessage/actor/outcome/version; 429 ErrorTooManyRequests with errorCode E0000047

official_sources.title: Monitoring and troubleshooting rate limits | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: X-Rate-Limit-Limit (ceiling for current request), X-Rate-Limit-Remaining (requests left in window), X-Rate-Limit-Reset (reset time in UTC epoch SECONDS); 429 on exceeding a bucket; recommendation to throttle/retry with backoff anchored on the reset value

official_sources.title: Core Okta API | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: SSWS Authorization scheme, org base URL pattern, cursor pagination via Link headers with the instruction to follow returned Link values because cursor formats can change without notice, error envelope errorCode/errorSummary/errorLink/errorId/errorCauses

research.language_rationale: The SIEM export fleet here is .NET services; Okta documents the System Log as plain REST with Link-header pagination and header-based rate limiting, so HttpClient + System.Text.Json against the documented wire contract is the idiomatic integration (the Okta .NET management SDK wraps this same OpenAPI surface and adds nothing for a single-endpoint exporter).

verified_facts: GET /api/v1/logs takes since, until, after, filter, q, limit (default 100, max 1000), sortOrder (ASCENDING default)
A polling query is ASCENDING with until unset; it always returns a Link rel="next" header, even when the page is empty; events are ordered by persistence time
The after cursor is opaque and system-generated; Okta's docs forbid crafting it — follow the returned next link verbatim because query params/cursor formats may change without notice
Manual pagination with since/until can skip or duplicate events; correct transfer follows next links only
Every response carries X-Rate-Limit-Limit, X-Rate-Limit-Remaining, and X-Rate-Limit-Reset (UTC epoch seconds)
Exceeding a bucket yields HTTP 429 with errorCode E0000047 ('API call exceeded rate limit due to too many requests.')
LogEvent carries uuid (unique event id), published (ISO 8601), eventType, severity, displayMessage, nested actor (id/type/alternateId/displayName) and outcome (result/reason)
Auth is Authorization: SSWS {token} (or OAuth Bearer with okta.logs.read); responses are application/json
